blocks.text: DTC P0125: Water temperature sensor tracking delay

The PGM-FI ECU supplies power (approximately 5 V) to the water temperature sensor signal circuit via a pull-up resistor. When the engine coolant temperature is low, the internal resistance of the water temperature sensor is high and the PGM-FI ECU detects a high signal voltage. When the engine coolant temperature is high, the internal resistance of the water temperature sensor is low and the PGM-FI ECU detects a low signal voltage. If the output voltage of the water temperature sensor does not reach the coolant temperature at which the theoretical AFR closed-loop control starts within the time set according to the engine coolant temperature at engine startup after engine startup, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

If the time until the engine coolant temperature [water temperature sensor] reaches -8°C is greater than or equal to the following table, depending on the engine coolant temperature [water temperature sensor] at engine startup.

rows: Engine coolant temperature at engine startup [water temperature sensor].

items.text: Start the engine below the engine coolant temperature [water temperature sensor] within the detection execution conditions.
Allow to stand idle for 20 minutes.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is judged to have a fault under conditions where the engine coolant temperature and intake air temperature at engine startup are within the specified values, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C under the same conditions as the first D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
